body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

transcript.text: for hello well it's time now so welcome everyone to the ITF madas uh session for this ITF 119 in Brisbane myself on Carlos and Carlos bernardos remote uh sharing the group so as usual this is an ITF meeting and uh as such uh Please be aware of the notwell and the the policies in effect that uh apply to these meetings so as a reminder when you participate in these meetings you are following the ipf process and policies your contribution will be covered by the applications that are own or controlled by your sponsor but you must disclose that in advance um the participation or attendee uh you acknowledge that written uh audio and video and photographic records will be made out of this meeting the information that you provide will be handled in accordance with the prop iatf privacy statement and please uh treat uh your peers respectfully uh and professionally if you have any uh further questions you can refer to the different BCPS on the screen um again this is intended for virtual uh uh and professional collaboration so we will be following the it of guidelines of conduct uh the anti-harassment policy the anti-harassment procedures and you have any concerns uh please talk to the utsman uh with uh in a confidential manner we will maintain a and and preserve an environment for uh treating everyone with dignity decency and respect so please do not engage in any harassment or hostile uh discussions this is um going to be recorded with Meo so please log onto the uh tool you have the QR code uh for local attendees so you can use your phone if you want to have a uh inperson uh attendance if you are using the full uh client uh we are going to be recording your attendance through through there and please do not share your audio and video unless you are actively participating we have here the the links to the agenda the meico and other information and if you have any questions uh please refer to to the different links okay so uh before we continue I'm going to make another call uh can someone help us uh take notes this is going to be a thanks thanks a lot B no worries we can do that as long as you take some high level once we we can go through the recordings but thank you very much appreciate it so we have uh some working group items that we want to discuss uh we're are going to have presentations for the first two uh the MAC address randomization current state of first document uh which we believe is ready for publication uh the use cases and identity requirements that is probably ready for working group last call and then we're going to have some discussion about the the informational uh Annex to replace the the BCP that was put put as U the uh one of the milestones for the group uh we also going to talk about the leas statement to ITF medinas and radex so um starting from the first one um I think we uh well we're going to have a a presentation probably we we want to have the discussion right after the presentation and Carlos will do the the update uh for the use cases and ID entity requirements we also got a a presentation from from Jerome in case he doesn't connect is because it's two o'clock for him so we may be able to present on his behalf we'll see if he if he shows up um and probably right after that we we can talk about the best current practices if that's okay okay so um moving on uh we received a another Le from the WBA uh which I'm writing here in a few slides uh in case you you didn't see it on the mailing list uh if you remember WBA is the institution that we've been uh Leasing and talking for a while uh notably on the open r discussions and um we they are the ones that are basically in charge of the open roaming Federation uh they gave some presentations uh they made um alas where they wanted to talk to us um and at the last uh meeting they actually came and participating came to the ATF meeting and participated in their open roaming hackathon where they were analyzing uh the concerns that we have about leakage of privac information and um ident identifiers that could fly uh and that could be picked up by by Third parties in this case the could be the network um or or the some some other third party so they they sent us as a this leas where they are sharing what has happened since and um well the first thing is that they they they have this work in the wrix um uh drop and roaming specifications where they are including normative text regarding uh end user privacy uh to prevent the unintentional weakening of end user um information being uh shared to to third parties um and this uh going on the on the radius uh access accept me messages and they are sharing this uh with both uh ourselves the working group medinas and radx so I believe there there could be some other discussions in radx to follow up on on this very specific issue um to be precise um they have these recommendations and I think this is an important outcome of the discussions and the work we've had because if you remember we we were raising some concerns about this privacy leakage so I'm going to just quickly read the WBA now recommends that the default identity provided policy should ensure that any correlation identifiers in the radius access accept messages such as class attribute 25 and or chargeable user identity attribute 89 are unique for each combination of end user and access network provider and that the uh keys and or in initialization vectors used in creating such Cor relation identifier should be refreshed at every 80 48 hours but not more frequently than every two hours the TW limit is assigned to permit the access network provider to perform autonomous troubleshooting of connectivity issues from authentic users that are repeatedly reinitiating connectivity to the access provider Network or and or permit the access provider identity uh to identify a m session originated by an authentic user device that has previously violated the open ring and user terms and conditions um in contrast to this default policy WBA wri iix specification describe scenarios where the 48 hour limit is required to be extended for example when the identity provider supports settle service and requires a correlation identifier to be stable over an entire billing period the WBA has work with the authors of open robing ID to the internet uh draft to update the draft to reflect these recent changes and you have a link there to the to the draft from Bruno Thomas they are planning to communicate these changes to open romic identity providers and uh they are basically presenting uh at radex so once again this is a positive outcome of the work that we were doing here uh when looking at Network Services and identification in the presence of uh randomized Mac addresses and how uh the studies that we did and the the work at the hackathon and our leasing with the WBA has uh already started Pro providing some good uh results and uh I don't know if uh Alan or someone wants to say something about radx or or how that uh it's going to be taken from here yeah this is Alan um the latest revision of the deprecating ins secure practices document in radex has some text about CLI that they' reviewed and said it meets their their needs I don't remember if it has those specific 48 hour issues or limitations but I'll check um but it seems to be pretty good and I believe they will be presenting at radex um and I've had a chat with some people I don't know that it would be a radex working group document the open roaming one at least um simply because it's documenting open realing but it should be reviewed by radex and then the individuals in radex would support it as an individual document of an need sponsor or something okay thanks a lot for that update okay so do we have any more uh questions or comments regarding this uh leason or the information about the the document all right so seeing none uh I'm going to stop sharing here and uh next item is Carlos okay hi everyone I hope you can hear me well uh yes can you hear me okay perfect so this is a short update on the randomizing Mac address so this revision number 12 just a very brief uh introduction or reminder of what is this draft about this draft is basically about documenting the current state of affairs regarding Mac add administation and what has been done in different sdos and what has been and is been done by different vendors of mostly mobile uh mobile phones but also desktop operating systems uh let me just go into the current table of content the table of content has changed I mean minor changes compared to the version that was presented in the last ATF basically we remove the the section on terminology that only had one one term that is very wellknown Mac Mac address term so we removed that there was a comment from met as part of the reviews about removing the table that we have from the current practices we decided not to do that because basically we did that already removing it and we put it back based on on working group feedback that uh basically the working Ro felt the consens was that um it was useful to have the curent snapshot even though this may change over time and that's the purpose of having the the link to the GitHub but so that's the reason we we maintain that table there and the rest has is basically the same as is used before regarding the same look since the last ATF we have we have done I think three or four iterations since last ATF uh mainly we got very good comments from Bob Ken and from uh from Med from Bob most related to yp6 um and the the way the interface identifier is generated the text that was in the draft originally was outdated so we updated that and I think Bob was happy with the changes but I guess he's there so probably he can comment if there is something that uh should remain an address from our side I hope it's not the case and also uh we received very good comments from met and uh we address most of them uh the only one that uh the only two that were not addressed was the title of the draft because I think this is this was also discussed in the past that LE in the charter and the tables as I mentioned before and we also got some additional comments uh and we believe now that the document is really uh ready for going to the next step so to it call and and final Publications hopefully so any comments questions if not the case Juan caros I guess we probably should uh request publication uh right after the the meeting right okay ER V the director in charge of madas so I would be so happy to accept this publication and start the process for going to the ATF last school and finally isg bot it has been two or three years now for this working group so it's time to to produce something thank you in advance okay thanks okay thanks Eric all right so so we'll Advance the the document there okay so if we have no more I don't think we have Jerome on the online so we're going to or or you no yeah so it's um very late for both of them um all right so so I'm going to present on their behalf I guess B uh just on the last one so what what are you what's happening you going to working group last call or what is the state of the previous document we're going to advance to to work to well it's already on working group last call so so it's in working group last call yes okay thank you okay so coming back to the to the Minas use cases document um so well here here is an update uh or showing uh the history of all the different updates that the authors have been uh addressing as you see uh as of the latest versions they they are they have been addressing the the different comments on the list um the the document has been stable quite uh for a while and it's uh probably uh getting ready for for working group uh last call too uh they included some specific uh details about the the changes probably we don't have to go into all the different details um for how they were addressing each one of the comments just going to skip the specifics and maybe this is um yeah so again the the document provides these use cases uh with the details about the the trust degree the type of network Administration the services that are expected and the network support uh expectation that uh is given so this was a baseline that that we used to to talk about the the different solutions and originally the intention was to take this uh Bas Baseline and then work on on the best current practices which as uh as we know we it hasn't been very successful we we don't have that best common practices but we have had some some good results and discussions with the WBA regarding existing Solutions but uh as of the lat latest discussions uh the solutions that were documented that were taken out of this document uh because we believe they would be moving to to a best common uh practices document uh are now in the limbo so some of the suggestions were to bring those uh current Solutions as an Annex as an informational Annex uh to back to this document so that uh assuming that we are not going to work on a best best uh BCP document uh we we could still recuperate the the documentation that we had on on those uh Solutions so trying to see here this this document this this uh page is still talking about the the the last changes that we're doing about the requirements but uh I guess what I just describe the section seven here existing Solutions was removed needs to be re added as an Annex and uh the authors are asking uh should we go for working group last call so I think this is this is what is uh remaining for this document I personally uh no no had uh believe that it makes sense to to bring back those uh sections as a as an Annex as an informational Annex just because it was a good follow up from from the table showing what are the existing solutions that that are there in the in the field that can be used to address the different use cases and uh without providing any specifics um I think it's good to have them in the in a in an informational Annex potentially referring to the work that will take place now in radex or at the WPA for instance um I don't know if anyone has any any comment or any feedback regarding This Way Forward Eric no specific command on the draft itself but at some point of time we will need a shefer as well for this document Peter was kind enough and thank you again to be the shefer for the other one but for this one we need one and there also an opportunity for somebody in the room where has never taken notor ship or cheffing or a chair position to try and understand a bit better the processes it's basically two three hours of work to be done within the next weeks thanks okay okay so assuming that we go that way uh is there anyone that could volunteer right now or or you prefer to discuss later on with the chairs up to you okay okay Peter thank you very much uh Peter e thank you very much okay so uh I mean we have I don't know if we have all the people here since this is a likely attended meeting uh what do you think should should we call for support for working group last call here at the meeting or maybe on the mailing list on the okay okay and if you do it right now it will be a three weeks because it's during the meeting we wait right now we wait three weeks we wait until Easter Monday and then you wait two weeks okay so I don't know if people remotely heard but uh so the suggestion is to go three weeks uh starting from now or wait after Easter and then do a two week working group last call in any case I think well since the authors are not here we need to provide them this feedback so probably makes sense to to wait okay fair enough so I think that's uh it and that is the the last contribution that we have for this meeting uh is there any other business or any other question that people would like to discuss for now okay so hearing now uh thank you very much everyone and uh enjoy the rest of your afternoon or your morning Carlos so do you expect to meet uh I guess we we can discuss in the M this but uh probably we don't need a a physical meeting we we will consider strongly not to meet physically in thanks everyone thank you guys bye thanks Carlos bye-bye thanks h okay thank you thank you
